prompts.label: New Next.js app (App Router)
prompts.text:
Create a beginner Next.js 14+ project with App Router.

- npx create-next-app steps (TypeScript, ESLint, Tailwind optional)
- Explain app/page.tsx and app/layout.tsx
- Change home page to Hello Next
- npm run dev and open localhost URL

List created folders in plain language.

prompts.label: Page and Link navigation
prompts.text:
Add a second page to a Next.js App Router project.

- app/about/page.tsx with title and paragraph
- Link from home using next/link
- Explain file-based routing

Provide full file contents.

prompts.label: Dynamic route [id]
prompts.text:
Teach dynamic routes in Next.js App Router.

- app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx
- Example slugs hello and world with different content
- Explain params prop type in TypeScript

Keep example minimal.

prompts.label: Server Component vs Client
prompts.text:
Explain Server vs Client Components for a beginner.

- Home as Server Component with static text
- Counter as Client Component with useState
- When to add 'use client'

Provide both files and 4 bullet comparison.

prompts.label: Fetch data in Server Component
prompts.text:
Fetch JSON in a Server Component (no useEffect).

- async function Page() fetching jsonplaceholder post
- Show loading not needed on server; mention error handling briefly
- TypeScript interface for Post

App Router only.

prompts.label: API Route handler GET
prompts.text:
Create a Route Handler GET /api/hello returning JSON.

- app/api/hello/route.ts
- Test with browser or curl
- Contrast with pages in 2 sentences

TypeScript.

prompts.label: Environment variables
prompts.text:
Explain env vars in Next.js for beginners.

- .env.local with NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME
- Use in server vs client components (rules)
- Never commit secrets

Show .env.example without real keys.

prompts.label: Metadata and page title
prompts.text:
Set page title and description using Metadata API.

- Static metadata on home page
- Explain why it matters for SEO

App Router syntax only.

prompts.label: Deploy to Vercel checklist
prompts.text:
Give a beginner checklist to deploy Next.js on Vercel.

- git push, import project, env vars on dashboard
- npm run build locally first
- Common build errors (types, env) in bullets

No account-specific steps.

prompts.label: Layout shared navbar
prompts.text:
Add a shared navbar in root layout.

- layout.tsx with html/body, nav links Home and About
- children slot for pages
- Minimal CSS or Tailwind if already enabled

Show full layout.tsx.

prompts.label: Route loading.tsx streaming skeleton
prompts.text:
Explain loading.tsx UX for suspense boundaries in the App Router.
Show how it relates to prefetching navigations mentally.
Compare putting skeletons globally vs granular route segments.
Warn about unintended layout jitter with tall skeletons.
Give a minimalist example component structure only.

prompts.label: error.tsx route boundary hygiene
prompts.text:
Teach local error.tsx boundaries versus global.
Demonstrate resetting error state with nested routes analogy.
List what errors propagate to boundaries vs silent failures logging only.
Connect to reporting services conceptually.
Mention distinguishing dev overlay vs prod user message.

prompts.label: not-found.tsx and notFound helper
prompts.text:
Differentiate triggering notFound() in server components versus static fallback pages.
Explain HTTP status expectation for bots vs SPA history.
Show tiny example dynamic segment missing record.
Relate metadata impact lightly.
Add SEO-friendly copy guidance for beginners.

prompts.label: generateStaticParams for static blogs
prompts.text:
Outline generateStaticParams returning slug list build-time.
Contrast with ISR revalidate timelines conceptually.
Explain what happens visiting unknown slug at runtime without dynamicParams tuning.
Mention drafting/unpublished workflows as CMS concern.
Keep code sketch short.

prompts.label: revalidateTag after mutations
prompts.text:
Explain fetch cache tagging and revalidateTag server usage after a form action.
Differentiate revalidatePath vs tag granularity.
Warn about eventual consistency wording for juniors.
Relate to headless CMS webhooks verbally.
Show pseudo flow only without vendor lock.

prompts.label: Server Actions mental sketch
prompts.text:
Explain Server Actions purpose without replacing full API design course.
Show mental model: form posts to server function with less client glue.
List limitations around browser API usage.
Discuss security validations still required.
Contrast with REST route handlers for public APIs.

prompts.label: next/image remotePatterns config
prompts.text:
Teach restricting remote image domains in next.config images.remotePatterns.
Explain why wildcard CDNs still need deliberate rules.
Mention widths/sizes interplay for responsiveness.
Show one mistake confusing static import vs URL string props.
Add note on placeholder blur data patterns high level.

prompts.label: middleware matcher auth gate
prompts.text:
Sketch middleware exporting config matcher guarding /dashboard routes.
Emphasize keeping checks fast and delegating DB work sparingly.
Explain redirect vs rewrite decisions simply.
Mention middleware runtime limitations cursory.
Caution beginners about leaking auth logic duplication with server checks.

prompts.label: Parallel routes dashboards concept
prompts.text:
Introduce parallel routes using @analytics and @team slots mentally.
Explain independent loading/error boundaries benefit.
Note complexity cost for small apps.
Give when not to adopt as a beginner.
Link idea to modals as parallel intercepting routes lightly without full tutorial.

prompts.label: Environment variables client vs server
prompts.text:
Clarify which env vars are exposed to the browser via NEXT_PUBLIC_.
Show rotation and preview deployment env matrix verbally.
Explain why secrets never belong in client bundles.
Describe runtime env with edge constraints briefly.
Give a checklist for debugging undefined env at build vs request time.
